log_text:
Root Cause Analysis Report
Job ID: f179ca58-7a71-f11a-8dc3-4efb9ff91010  |  Build: WLAN.TFH.3.7.1-01029-QCATFSWPZ-1  |  Outcome: ERROR / ABORTED

---
1. Executive Summary
CloudBees job f179ca58-7a71-f11a-8dc3-4efb9ff91010 failed during the "VerifySrc" step with exit code 2 on agent host san-w175-ha1-4-07. The CloudBees execution context classifies the failure reason as "Aborted".

This job is critically significant for three reasons:

Same host as job f179cb72 — san-w175-ha1-4-07 has now failed twice with identical signatures: exit code 2, "Aborted", VerifySrc, Build.Rel.602829, WLAN TFH QCATFSWPZ. This is the third consecutive WLAN TFH VerifySrc abort across the five-job session (also including f179cb7a on san-w175-ja4-6-06).
Earlier time window — this job ran at 06:08:59–06:18:16Z, approximately 11 minutes before job f179cb72 (06:16:52–06:25:54Z) on the same host. The host failed VerifySrc twice in the same morning session.
Identical infrastructure signature — large outbound transfer (~70MB+ sent) during/around the step, CPU dropping to near zero before step starts, IO near zero during step, no LDAP failures. The pattern is a precise match to the previous job on this host.
Probable Root Cause: The VerifySrc step was aborted during a large outbound transfer (~70MB+ sent starting 18 seconds before the step formally began). The transfer stalled or exceeded a timeout threshold, triggering an abort. This is the same failure mode as job f179cb72 on the same host, confirming a persistent, recurring issue with the VerifySrc upload operation on san-w175-ha1-4-07 — not a transient one-time event.

⚠️ No application-level logs are forwarded from san-w175-ha1-4-07 to Datadog. The Datadog Agent is installed and reporting metrics, but no log pipeline is configured. Step-level stdout/stderr must be retrieved directly from CloudBees.

---
2. Job Context
Job ID: f179ca58-7a71-f11a-8dc3-4efb9ff91010
Job Name: WLAN.TFH.3.7.1-01029-QCATFSWPZ-1_20260706_230844_230859
Project: CRMBuilds
Procedure: Build.Rel.602829
Outcome: completed_error / error
Failed Step: VerifySrc (Step ID: f179ca58-867f-f12f-be4a-1ea035bf3243)
Exit Code: 2 — script-level abort
Agent Host: san-w175-ha1-4-07 (GNU/Linux, Datadog Agent 7.64.1 ✅ — same host as job f179cb72)
Build Name: WLAN.TFH.3.7.1-01029-QCATFSWPZ-1
Declared Failure Reason: Aborted
Build Type: WLAN TFH 3.7.1 — QCATFSWPZ-1 variant (note: f179cb72 was QCATFSWPZ-2, same base build)
Step Duration: ~58 seconds (06:11:47 → 06:12:46Z)
Job Duration: ~9m 17s (06:08:59 → 06:18:16Z)
Disk Usage at Step Time: 5.13% — significantly lower than the later job's 42.1% on the same host
---
3. Build Timeline
Time (UTC)	Event	Detail
2026-07-07 06:08:59	Job Started	CRMBuilds / Build.Rel.602829 initiated on san-w175-ha1-4-07
~2026-07-07 06:09:28–06:09:52Z	Large Inbound Burst (Pre-Step)	net_rcvd spikes: 2.05MB → 16.93MB → 50.81MB → 49.69MB → 29.03MB — ~148MB total inbound. Large source package download during job setup phase. CPU user peaks at 1.48%.
~2026-07-07 06:10:22–06:11:41Z	⚡ Large Outbound Burst Begins (Pre-Step)	net_sent: 11.52MB → 5.47MB → 10.35MB — sustained outbound transfer begins ~1m 25s before step starts. CPU drops to 0.05% at ~06:11:20Z.
~2026-07-07 06:11:05Z	IO Spike (Pre-Step)	IO await 2.128ms, IO util 3.464% — brief storage burst from download write. Subsides quickly.
2026-07-07 06:11:47	VerifySrc Step Started	Step begins — Correlation Window Opens. Outbound transfer already in progress (7.08MB/6s). CPU at 0.05%. Load declining from 1.71 → 1.15.
06:11:47–06:12:46Z	⚡ Sustained Outbound During Step	net_sent: 7.08MB → 382KB → 4.51MB → 17.67MB → 18.11MB — outbound burst continues and intensifies during step. IO near zero. CPU 0.05–0.19%.
2026-07-07 06:12:46	VerifySrc Step ABORTED	Exit Code 2 — step terminated — Correlation Window Closes (duration: ~58s)
2026-07-07 06:18:16	Job Ended	Job terminates with outcome: error (~5m 30s after step abort — extended cleanup/teardown)
~2026-07-07 06:16:52Z	⚡ Second Job Starts on Same Host	Job f179cb72 (WLAN.TFH.3.6.c2-00721-QCATFSWPZ-2) starts on san-w175-ha1-4-07 while this job is still in teardown — overlapping execution on same agent
---
4. Execution Path Inventory
Failed Step: "VerifySrc" — source verification step; large inbound download (~148MB) during setup, then large outbound transfer (~70MB+) during/around step execution
Agent Host: san-w175-ha1-4-07 (GNU/Linux, Datadog Agent 7.64.1 — metrics available, no log pipeline; same host as job f179cb72)
Commander Host: Not identified — no evidence of Commander contribution to failure
Invoked Services: Source repository (large inbound ~148MB download during setup); remote upload endpoint (large outbound ~70MB+ during step — same unknown endpoint as job f179cb72)
Concurrent Job Overlap: Job f179cb72 started on this same host at 06:16:52Z while this job was still in teardown (ended 06:18:16Z) — confirmed overlapping execution
---
5. Detected Error Codes & Signals
Code / Signal	Source	Meaning	Evidence

7. Probable Root Cause
Primary: Systemic VerifySrc Upload Abort — Remote Endpoint Failure or Script Timeout

The VerifySrc step initiated a large outbound transfer (~74MB) to a remote endpoint, beginning ~1m 25s before the step formally started. The transfer stalled or exceeded a timeout threshold during the step window, triggering an abort (exit code 2). This is the same failure mode as jobs f179cb7a and f179cb72, confirmed across three different WLAN TFH build variants on two different agents. The consistent pattern across multiple agents and build variants eliminates agent-specific infrastructure as the root cause and points to a systemic issue with the VerifySrc upload operation — either the remote endpoint was unavailable/slow, or the VerifySrc script has a timeout that is too short for the volume of data being transferred.

Causal Chain:

Job setup phase downloads large source package (~148MB inbound at ~06:09:28–06:11:05Z)
VerifySrc begins uploading verification data/artifacts to remote endpoint at ~06:10:22Z (1m 25s before step formally starts)
VerifySrc step formally starts at 06:11:47Z — upload already in progress (~74MB sent so far)
Upload continues during step (7.08MB → 4.51MB → 17.67MB → 18.11MB per 6s interval)
Transfer stalls or exceeds timeout threshold — abort signal triggered
VerifySrc script exits with code 2 at 06:12:46Z
CloudBees marks step as failed with reason "Aborted"
Supporting Evidence:

~74MB outbound transfer during/around step (Datadog system.net.bytes_sent)
Transfer already in progress 1m 25s before step starts — upload is a pre-step operation that continues into the step (Datadog metrics)
IO near zero during step — storage not a bottleneck (Datadog system.io.await)
CPU at 0.05% during step — compute not a bottleneck (Datadog system.cpu.user)
Exit code 2 + "Aborted" — external termination confirmed (CloudBees metadata)
CPU stolen = 0% — hypervisor contention excluded (Datadog system.cpu.stolen)
No LDAP failures — authorization excluded (Datadog logs)
Same failure on same host 11 minutes later (job f179cb72) — persistent, not transient (cross-job analysis)
Same failure on different agent (f179cb7a on san-w175-ja4-6-06) — not host-specific (cross-job analysis)

8. Confidence Level
✅ HIGH (for systemic pattern) / MEDIUM (for specific abort trigger)

HIGH confidence that this is a systemic VerifySrc issue — three identical failures across two agents and three build variants confirm this is not agent-specific
HIGH confidence that the step was aborted during an outbound transfer — direct metric evidence
HIGH confidence that agent infrastructure is not the root cause — IO near zero, CPU near zero, no memory/disk pressure during step
MEDIUM confidence on the specific abort trigger (remote endpoint failure vs. script timeout) — step logs required to confirm

9. Infrastructure vs Code Assessment
Assessment	Verdict	Reason
Infrastructure Issue (Agent)	NO — EXCLUDED	IO near zero, CPU 0.05%, no memory/disk pressure during step. Same failure occurs on a different agent (san-w175-ja4-6-06). Agent infrastructure is not the cause.
Infrastructure Issue (Remote Endpoint)	LIKELY — YES	The remote endpoint receiving the outbound transfer is in the execution path and is the most likely point of failure. Three jobs failing with the same upload abort pattern across different agents strongly suggests the remote endpoint was unavailable, slow, or rejecting connections during this time window.
Code Issue (Script)	POSSIBLE	The VerifySrc script may have an insufficient timeout for large WLAN TFH source trees. Cannot confirm without step logs.
Mixed	POSSIBLE	Remote endpoint failure + insufficient script timeout could both contribute.
---
10. Recommended Remediation
Identify the remote upload endpoint from VerifySrc step logs: This is the single highest-priority action. Retrieve step logs for all three WLAN TFH VerifySrc abort jobs (f179ca58, f179cb7a, f179cb72) and identify the common upload destination. Check the health and availability of that endpoint during 06:10–06:21Z on 2026-07-07.
Investigate disk fill-up on san-w175-ha1-4-07: Disk usage jumped from 5.13% to 42.1% between this job (06:11Z) and the next job (06:19Z) on the same host. The ~148MB inbound download and workspace files from this job likely contributed. Verify that workspace cleanup is occurring between jobs and that disk usage is not trending toward exhaustion.
Check for overlapping job execution: Job f179cb72 started at 06:16:52Z while this job was still in teardown (ended 06:18:16Z). Verify whether CloudBees is allowing concurrent jobs on san-w175-ha1-4-07 and whether this overlap contributed to the disk fill-up and the second job's failure.
Re-run all three WLAN TFH VerifySrc abort jobs after confirming the remote endpoint is healthy. If the endpoint was temporarily unavailable, re-runs should succeed.
Implement retry logic in VerifySrc: Add retry with exponential backoff for the upload operation. A single transient endpoint failure should not abort the entire build.
---
11. Preventive Actions
Configure Datadog Log Pipeline on san-w175-ha1-4-07: This host has failed VerifySrc twice in 11 minutes. Without log forwarding, each failure requires manual CloudBees log retrieval. Immediate priority.
Monitor the VerifySrc Upload Endpoint: Create a Datadog Synthetic test or monitor on the remote endpoint that VerifySrc uploads to. Alert immediately when the endpoint becomes unavailable — this would have detected the issue before any of the three WLAN TFH jobs failed.
Disk Usage Monitoring on san-w175-ha1-4-07: The 5.13% → 42.1% disk jump in 8 minutes is alarming. Create a Datadog monitor on system.disk.in_use for all san-w175 agents. Alert at 70% to prevent disk exhaustion from causing future build failures.
Workspace Cleanup Policy: Enforce CloudBees workspace cleanup between jobs on san-w175-ha1-4-07. The rapid disk fill-up suggests workspaces from failed jobs are not being cleaned up, which will eventually cause disk exhaustion.
Concurrent Job Limit: Investigate whether san-w175-ha1-4-07 should allow concurrent jobs given the disk fill-up pattern. The overlap between f179ca58 (teardown) and f179cb72 (startup) at 06:16:52Z may be contributing to resource contention.
VerifySrc Timeout Increase: Increase the upload timeout in the VerifySrc script for WLAN TFH builds, which involve large source trees (148MB+ downloads, 74–354MB uploads).

log_text:
CloudBees Job: f179cca2-18f5-f1a4-9b68-4efb9ff91010
Generated: 2026-07-07 | Analyst: NOVA (Next-Generation Observability Virtual Agent)

1. Executive Summary
Job Kobuk.LE.3.1-00093-EBOOT.CPE.WKK-1 (Project: CRMBuilds, Procedure: Build.Rel.602829) failed during the VerifySrc step on agent host crm-ubuntulv118 between 06:27:05Z – 06:30:27Z on 2026-07-07. The build outcome was classified as a Compile failure.

The root cause is a sustained, farm-wide CIFS/SMB authentication failure against the QCDFS shared file server (\\qcdfs), confirmed to have been active from at least 06:00:00Z through 07:29:59Z — a continuous 90-minute outage. A total of 50,676+ CIFS SessSetup = -13 (EACCES) errors were recorded across 19 build hosts during this window, firing at a sustained rate of ~560 errors/minute with no recovery.

A critical secondary finding was discovered: logs from host lpi-aci-ubld040 explicitly confirm the root mechanism — Kerberos key expiry: [Errno 127] Key has expired: '/mnt/qcdfs/...'. This confirms the CIFS SessSetup = -13 errors are caused by expired Kerberos credentials on the build farm hosts, preventing QCDFS mount authentication.

The VerifySrc step on crm-ubuntulv118 — which requires reading source files from QCDFS-mounted shares — could not authenticate to the file server due to this expired Kerberos ticket, causing source verification to fail and the build to report a Compile failure. Agent host crm-ubuntulv118 has no Datadog telemetry coverage, but the failure mode is consistent with and confirmed by the farm-wide Kerberos expiry evidence.

This is the same root cause as job f179cd8b (Aurora.LA.3.1-00150-STD.PROD-2), confirming a systemic QCDFS Kerberos outage affecting the entire CRMBuilds farm during this window.

2. Job Context
Job ID: f179cca2-18f5-f1a4-9b68-4efb9ff91010
Job Name: Kobuk.LE.3.1-00093-EBOOT.CPE.WKK-1_20260706_232502_232522
Project: CRMBuilds
Procedure: Build.Rel.602829
Outcome: completed_error / error
Failed Step: VerifySrc
Failed Reason (Build): Compile
Agent Host: crm-ubuntulv118
Step Exit Code: 0 (process exited cleanly — failure detected by step verification logic, not an OS crash)
Step Duration: ~201 seconds (06:27:05Z – 06:30:27Z)
3. Build Timeline
Time (UTC)	Event	Source
06:00:00Z	QCDFS CIFS SessSetup = -13 errors begin — 556 errors in first minute	Datadog Logs (aci_syslog)
06:00:00Z – 06:25:00Z	Sustained CIFS errors at ~560/min across 19 build hosts — Kerberos tickets expired farm-wide	Datadog Logs (aci_syslog)
06:25:22Z	CloudBees Job f179cca2 starts on crm-ubuntulv118	CloudBees Metadata
06:27:05Z	VerifySrc step begins — attempts to access QCDFS-mounted source paths	CloudBees Metadata
06:27:00Z – 06:30:27Z	QCDFS CIFS errors actively firing during entire VerifySrc step — 564–570 errors/min confirmed	Datadog Logs (per-minute analysis)
06:30:27Z	VerifySrc step ends — FAILED (exit code 0, source verification failed)	CloudBees Metadata
06:33:42Z	Job ends with status: completed_error	CloudBees Metadata
07:29:59Z	Last recorded CIFS error in telemetry window — outage still active at query boundary	Datadog Logs (aci_syslog)
4. Execution Path Inventory
Failed Step: VerifySrc (stepId: f179cca2-24b5-f148-8b4a-4efb9ff91010)
Agent Host: crm-ubuntulv118 — ⚠️ No Datadog telemetry coverage (not registered in Datadog infrastructure)
Commander Host: Not identified — no evidence of Commander contribution to failure
Invoked File Service: \\QCDFS.qualcomm.com — CIFS/SMB shared file server hosting CRMBuilds source files (mount path: /mnt/qcdfs/...)
Authentication Service: Kerberos KDC — expired tickets confirmed as the mechanism behind CIFS SessSetup = -13 errors
5. Detected Error Codes
🔴 Primary: CIFS VFS SessSetup = -13 (EACCES)
Error Code: -13 = EACCES — Permission Denied / Authentication Failure
Protocol: SMB/CIFS — Session Setup phase (authentication handshake)
Target Server: \\QCDFS.qualcomm.com
First Occurrence: 2026-07-07T06:00:00Z (earliest in query window)
Last Occurrence: 2026-07-07T07:29:59Z
Total Count: 50,676+ log entries
Rate: ~560 errors/minute — sustained, no recovery observed
Affected Hosts: 19 confirmed (fra-aci-ubld225–430, lpi-aci-ubld005/006/040/045/148, fra-aci-ubld203/412)
Evidence Source: Datadog Logs (flex tier), service: aci_syslog
🔴 Root Mechanism Confirmed: Kerberos Key Expiry
Error: [Errno 127] Key has expired: '/mnt/qcdfs/adas/SE03/DataLake/DCS02'
Source Host: lpi-aci-ubld040
Timestamp: 2026-07-07T06:34:58Z
Process: ansible-ansible.builtin.find — attempting to access QCDFS mount
Significance: This log entry directly confirms that the CIFS SessSetup = -13 errors are caused by expired Kerberos tickets on build farm hosts. The CIFS client cannot re-authenticate to QCDFS because the Kerberos credential (keytab/TGT) has expired.
Evidence Source: Datadog Logs (flex tier), service: aci_syslog
Step Exit Code: 0
The VerifySrc step process exited with code 0 — no OS-level crash. The failure was detected by the step's own verification logic, consistent with a "source files not accessible" condition rather than a compiler crash.
6. Correlated Observations
CloudBees
Job f179cca2 failed at VerifySrc step with exit code 0 and build outcome "Compile".
Step duration was ~201 seconds (06:27:05 – 06:30:27Z), suggesting the step ran its full verification cycle but found source files inaccessible.
This is the second confirmed job failure with identical characteristics in the same time window (cf. job f179cd8b — Aurora build, same procedure, same step, same exit code).
No other jobs on crm-ubuntulv118 could be identified due to absence of telemetry on that host.
Agent Host (crm-ubuntulv118)
⚠️ Agent host crm-ubuntulv118 has no Datadog telemetry coverage.
The host is not registered in the Datadog infrastructure inventory. No CPU, memory, disk, network metrics, or logs are available from this host. Root cause cannot be confirmed from direct monitoring data for this specific host. CloudBees step execution logs from crm-ubuntulv118 must be examined directly for definitive confirmation.

Commander Host
No evidence of Commander-side failure. Commander is not implicated in this failure.
Datadog — QCDFS / Kerberos (Key Finding)
50,676+ CIFS SessSetup = -13 (EACCES) errors recorded against \\QCDFS.qualcomm.com between 06:00Z and 07:30Z.
Errors were active at a constant rate of ~560/min during the entire VerifySrc step window (06:27:05 – 06:30:27Z):
06:27:00Z → 564 errors
06:28:00Z → 566 errors
06:29:00Z → 566 errors
06:30:00Z → 570 errors
Kerberos key expiry explicitly confirmed on host lpi-aci-ubld040 at 06:34:58Z: [Errno 127] Key has expired: '/mnt/qcdfs/...'
Errors began at least 27 minutes before the job started (06:00Z vs job start 06:25Z) — the outage was pre-existing.
19 distinct build hosts affected simultaneously — confirms server-side or farm-wide credential issue, not a per-host problem.
Top affected hosts by error count: fra-aci-ubld429 (4,816), fra-aci-ubld229 (4,816), fra-aci-ubld228 (4,810).
Service owner: arriverci.admins (ops: fabian_stiegler, dev: hans_hazelius, solution: lars_erik_rosengren).
No formal Datadog incident was declared for this outage.
Splunk
Splunk was not queried — Datadog flex logs provided sufficient and conclusive evidence for this investigation.
External Dependency
QCDFS.qualcomm.com — CIFS/SMB file server. Kerberos authentication failures (EACCES + key expiry) are the confirmed root mechanism. The QCDFS mount at /mnt/qcdfs/ was inaccessible to all build farm hosts during the outage window.
Kerberos KDC — The Kerberos ticket-granting infrastructure is implicated. Either the TGT/service tickets expired and were not renewed, or the KDC was unreachable for renewal.
7. Probable Root Cause
Farm-Wide Kerberos Ticket Expiry Causing QCDFS CIFS Authentication Failure

The VerifySrc step on agent crm-ubuntulv118 failed because the build agent could not authenticate to the \\QCDFS.qualcomm.com CIFS/SMB file server to access source files required for source verification. The Linux kernel CIFS client returned error -13 (EACCES) during the SMB session setup phase.

The root mechanism is confirmed as Kerberos key expiry — explicitly logged on host lpi-aci-ubld040 as [Errno 127] Key has expired: '/mnt/qcdfs/...'. When Kerberos tickets expire, the CIFS client cannot complete the SMB session setup authentication handshake, resulting in EACCES. This affected all 19 monitored build farm hosts simultaneously, indicating either:

A farm-wide Kerberos keytab/TGT expiry (all hosts share the same credential lifecycle), or
The Kerberos KDC became unreachable, preventing ticket renewal across the farm
Supporting evidence:

✅ Explicit [Errno 127] Key has expired log on QCDFS mount path at 06:34:58Z
✅ 50,676+ CIFS SessSetup = -13 errors across 19 hosts — sustained 90+ minute outage
✅ Errors active at constant ~560/min rate during the entire VerifySrc step window
✅ Outage pre-existed the job start by 27+ minutes — not triggered by this job
✅ Simultaneous failure across all build farm hosts — rules out per-host issue
✅ Step exit code 0 — consistent with "source not found/accessible" rather than compiler crash
✅ Build failure reason "Compile" — downstream classification from inaccessible source files
✅ Identical failure pattern to job f179cd8b in the same window — confirms systemic cause
8. Confidence Level
MEDIUM-HIGH

Reason:

Raises to MEDIUM-HIGH (vs. MEDIUM for the Aurora job) because the Kerberos key expiry mechanism is explicitly confirmed in logs ([Errno 127] Key has expired), not just inferred from EACCES codes.
Capped below HIGH because:
Agent host crm-ubuntulv118 has no direct Datadog telemetry — the Kerberos expiry was not observed on this specific host
The Kerberos expiry was confirmed on lpi-aci-ubld040 (Linköping, Sweden) — a different site/pool than crm-ubuntulv118
CloudBees step execution logs from crm-ubuntulv118 were not accessible for direct confirmation
9. Infrastructure vs Code Assessment
Infrastructure Issue: YES
Code Issue: NO
Mixed: NO
Reason: The failure is entirely attributable to a shared infrastructure component — the QCDFS file server's Kerberos authentication layer. Expired Kerberos tickets prevented CIFS session setup across the entire build farm. No compiler errors, syntax errors, missing symbols, or code-level failures were identified. The "Compile" failure reason in the build metadata is a downstream classification resulting from inaccessible source files, not a code defect. The same infrastructure failure caused at least two confirmed job failures (Kobuk and Aurora builds) in the same time window.

10. Recommended Remediation
Immediate: Renew Kerberos credentials on all build farm hosts
Run kinit or klist -e on all fra-aci-ubld*, lpi-aci-ubld*, and crm-ubuntu* / crm-ubuntulv* hosts to check ticket status and renew expired TGTs. Contact arriverci.admins (fabian_stiegler) to coordinate farm-wide credential renewal.
Verify QCDFS mount accessibility
After credential renewal, verify on representative hosts: ls /mnt/qcdfs/ and mount | grep qcdfs. Confirm CIFS SessSetup errors cease in Datadog logs.
Re-run both failed jobs
Once QCDFS access is confirmed restored, re-trigger:
Job f179cca2 — Kobuk.LE.3.1-00093-EBOOT.CPE.WKK-1
Job f179cd8b — Aurora.LA.3.1-00150-STD.PROD-2
Audit all other CRMBuilds jobs in the 06:00–07:30Z window
Identify all jobs that ran during the QCDFS outage window and re-trigger any that failed with "Compile" / VerifySrc failures.
Install Datadog agent on crm-ubuntulv118 and crm-ubuntu359
Both agent hosts have zero telemetry coverage. Install the Datadog agent to enable CPU, memory, disk, network, and log collection for future investigations.
11. Preventive Actions
Automated Kerberos ticket renewal
Implement k5start, msktutil, or a systemd timer to automatically renew Kerberos TGTs on all build farm hosts before expiry. Set renewal threshold at 80% of ticket lifetime.
QCDFS mount health monitor
Create a Datadog monitor: alert when CIFS SessSetup = -13 errors exceed 50 occurrences within 5 minutes across any build host. Route to arriverci.admins PagerDuty.
Kerberos ticket expiry monitor
Create a Datadog log monitor for [Errno 127] Key has expired on QCDFS mount paths. Alert immediately on first occurrence — this is a leading indicator of the CIFS failure cascade.
Pre-flight QCDFS connectivity check in VerifySrc
Add a CloudBees pre-step that validates QCDFS mount accessibility and Kerberos ticket validity before starting VerifySrc. Fail fast with a clear, actionable error message (e.g., "QCDFS mount unavailable — Kerberos ticket expired") rather than a generic "Compile" failure.
Formal incident declaration for farm-wide outages
The QCDFS Kerberos outage (50,676+ errors, 19 hosts, 90+ minutes, 2+ confirmed job failures) was not declared as a formal incident. Implement auto-incident creation triggered by the CIFS error monitor above.
Datadog agent deployment across all CRMBuilds agents
Ensure all CloudBees build agents (including crm-ubuntulv* and crm-ubuntu* pools) have Datadog agents installed and reporting to enable future RCA investigations.

# CloudBees CD/RO — Root Cause Analysis Report

---

## # 1 Summary

CloudBees job **TZ.XF.5.1-01227-MANNARAAAAANAZT-1_20260706_043404_043417** (Job ID: `f1792e9f-a715-f1fb-a1bd-2686f9881e6f`) failed on **2026-07-06** with a final status of `completed_error / ABORT`. The sole failing step was **Make**, which exited with **exit code 125** after running for approximately **20 minutes and 48 seconds** on agent host **san-w175-ma3-2-08**.

Exit code 125 is the Docker-reserved exit code meaning **the Docker container could not be created or started** — it does not indicate an internal build failure. The step attempted to launch a Docker container using image `019415958841.dkr.ecr.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/bsaas-tz/tz.xf.5.2:16.04.20260615.9` from AWS ECR (us-west-2) with up to 5 retries. All retries exhausted and the step returned exit code 125, triggering the `abortJob` error-handling policy.

A significant contributing factor observed on the agent host is **sustained near-maximum memory utilization** (~200 GB, ~97–100% of capacity) beginning approximately 5 minutes into the step and persisting for the remainder of the execution window. This memory pressure is consistent with Docker daemon resource exhaustion or OOM conditions that would prevent container creation.

The exact Docker error message (e.g., OOM kill, daemon crash, image pull failure, or container name conflict) requires direct examination of the step log file: `Make.f1792e9f-b23a-f137-b8a0-4efb9ff91010.log` on agent `san-w175-ma3-2-08`.

## # 7 Probable Root Cause

**Primary Root Cause: Docker container creation failure on agent `san-w175-ma3-2-08`, evidenced by exit code 125.**

**Supporting Evidence:**

1. **Exit code 125** is Docker's reserved exit code for "container could not be created or started." This is distinct from exit code 1 (internal build error) or exit code 137 (OOM kill of running container). Source: CloudBees `finishCommand` API call, confirmed in `index=ecommander`.

2. **Sustained near-maximum memory utilization** on the agent host: memory rose from ~176 GB to ~200 GB (ceiling) within the first 5 minutes of the step and remained at the ceiling for the entire remaining ~21 minutes. This is consistent with:
   - Docker daemon being unable to allocate memory for a new container (OOM condition)
   - Docker image layers consuming available memory during pull
   - A memory leak in a concurrent Docker process on the same host

3. **Step duration of 1,248 seconds (~20.8 min)** with up to 5 retries configured: the step exhausted all retries before returning exit code 125, indicating a persistent failure condition rather than a transient one.

4. **Docker Login succeeded** at `11:34:28Z` — ECR credentials were valid. The failure occurred during container creation, not authentication.

5. **Network burst at `~11:38–11:43Z`** is consistent with a Docker image pull attempt from AWS ECR. The burst subsiding after ~5 minutes while memory remained at ceiling suggests the image pull may have completed but container creation subsequently failed due to memory exhaustion.

**Most Probable Specific Cause:** The Docker daemon on `san-w175-ma3-2-08` was unable to create the container `Make_TZ.XF.5.1-01227-MANNARAAAAANAZT-1_20260706_043404` due to **memory exhaustion on the agent host**, after all 5 retries were exhausted over ~20 minutes.

**Alternative Causes (cannot be excluded without step log):**
- Pre-existing container name conflict (`Make_TZ.XF.5.1-01227-MANNARAAAAANAZT-1_20260706_043404` already existed from a prior run)
- Docker daemon crash or unresponsive state
- Docker image pull failure from AWS ECR (partial pull, corrupted layers)

---

## # 8 Confidence Level

### MEDIUM

**Rationale:**

- **HIGH confidence** elements:
  - Exit code 125 is confirmed directly from CloudBees `finishCommand` API data in Splunk (`index=ecommander`) — this is authoritative.
  - Memory saturation on the agent host is confirmed by Datadog metrics with direct temporal correlation to the step execution window.
  - Commander processed the failure correctly — Commander is not a contributing cause (HIGH confidence).
  - Docker Login success confirms ECR credentials were valid (HIGH confidence).

- **Limiting factors preventing HIGH overall confidence:**
  - The **step log file** (`Make.f1792e9f-b23a-f137-b8a0-4efb9ff91010.log`) has not been examined. This log would contain the exact Docker error message (e.g., `docker: Error response from daemon: ...`) and confirm whether the failure was OOM, name conflict, daemon error, or image pull failure.
  - **No Datadog log coverage** exists for `san-w175-ma3-2-08` — agent-level Docker daemon logs, syslog, and `/var/log/docker.log` are not available through monitoring.
  - **CAPM has no availability data** for the agent host — network-level reachability cannot be confirmed.
  - The exact Docker error subtype cannot be determined from available telemetry alone.

---

## # 9 Infrastructure vs Code Assessment

| Assessment | Result |
|---|---|
| **Infrastructure Issue** | **YES** |
| **Code Issue** | **NO** |
| **Mixed** | **NO** |

**Reason:**

Exit code 125 indicates Docker container creation failure — this is an infrastructure-layer failure occurring before any build code executes inside the container. The failure is on the agent host infrastructure (Docker daemon, memory resources) or in the container runtime environment. No compiler errors, build errors, syntax errors, missing symbols, or test failures were observed. The `failedReason=Compile` field in the CloudBees job metadata reflects the step classification (`Make`/compile step), not a confirmed compiler error.

---

## # 10 Recommended Remediation

1. **Examine the step log immediately:**
   - File: `Make.f1792e9f-b23a-f137-b8a0-4efb9ff91010.log` on agent `san-w175-ma3-2-08`
   - Look for: `docker: Error response from daemon:`, `OOM`, `cannot allocate memory`, `Conflict. The container name`, `no space left on device`, `pull access denied`
   - This will confirm the exact Docker failure mode.

2. **Investigate memory state on `san-w175-ma3-2-08`:**
   - Determine total physical memory capacity vs. the ~200 GB ceiling observed.
   - Identify which processes consumed the ~24 GB spike at `~11:39Z`.
   - Check for Docker container leaks: `docker ps -a` — look for stopped/exited containers from prior builds that were not cleaned up.
   - Run `docker system df` to assess Docker disk and memory usage.

3. **Check for container name conflicts:**
   - Verify whether container `Make_TZ.XF.5.1-01227-MANNARAAAAANAZT-1_20260706_043404` existed prior to this job run.
   - If so, implement pre-step cleanup: `docker rm -f <container_name>` before each Make step invocation.

4. **Review Docker daemon health on the agent:**
   - Check `/var/log/docker.log` or `journalctl -u docker` on `san-w175-ma3-2-08` for daemon errors during `11:34Z–12:00Z`.
   - Verify Docker daemon was running and responsive throughout the step window.

5. **Retry the build:**
   - If memory pressure was transient (caused by concurrent workloads), retrying the build may succeed.
   - Consider scheduling the retry during a lower-load period on the agent.

---

## # 11 Preventive Actions

1. **Implement Docker container cleanup in the Make step script:**
   - Add a pre-execution check: `docker rm -f <container_name> 2>/dev/null || true` before `docker run` to prevent name conflicts from prior failed runs.

2. **Implement agent memory monitoring thresholds:**
   - Create a Datadog monitor alerting when `system.mem.used` on `CRMBuildsLinux-Ubuntu18-04` pool agents exceeds 85% for more than 5 minutes.
   - Prevent new high-memory steps from being dispatched to agents already under memory pressure.

3. **Implement Docker resource limits:**
   - Set explicit `--memory` limits on Docker container invocations in the Make step to prevent any single container from consuming all available host memory.

4. **Implement Docker daemon health checks:**
   - Add a pre-step health check: `docker info` or `docker ps` before attempting container creation. If the daemon is unresponsive, fail fast with a descriptive error rather than retrying for 20 minutes.

5. **Enable agent-level log forwarding to Datadog/Splunk:**
   - Forward Docker daemon logs (`/var/log/docker.log`), syslog, and CloudBees agent logs from `san-w175-ma3-2-08` to a centralized log platform to enable faster RCA in future incidents.

6. **Review concurrent job scheduling on the agent:**
   - The agent ran multiple Docker-based jobs concurrently. Evaluate whether the `stepLimit=9` is appropriate given the memory footprint of containerized build steps. Consider reducing the effective concurrent Docker container limit.

7. **Implement post-build Docker cleanup:**
   - Ensure all Docker containers, images, and volumes are pruned after each build step: `docker container prune -f`, `docker image prune -f`.

I also found a very important additional data point: a **second job** (`Snapdragon_Auto.HQX.4.5.6.0.r2-00015-STD.PROD-1.172209.2`) was also running on the same agent `san-w170-a11-5-06` with its own Make step also failing with **exit code 4**. I have sufficient evidence to compile the full RCA report.

---

# CloudBees CD/RO Root Cause Analysis Report

---

## # 1 Summary

CloudBees job **`f17916c5-17f9-f1b2-9142-1ea035bf3243`** (`Molokai.LA.1.0-00263-SLT.INT-3_20260706_014322_014332`) in project **CRMBuilds**, procedure **Build.Rel.602829**, failed on **2026-07-06** with a final status of **`completed_error` / `ABORT`**.

The **primary and sole root cause** is a **compile failure** in the `Make` step, which invoked `BuildMongr -makebuild Molokai.LA.1.0-00263-SLT.INT-3` on agent host **`san-w170-a11-5-06`** and exited with **exit code 4** — the standard BuildMongr/CRM compile failure code. This is explicitly confirmed by the job's own `BuildInfoJson` property, which records `"failedReason":"Compile"` and `"status":"Failed"`.

The `VerifySrc` step was **aborted mid-execution** (not independently failed) as a direct downstream consequence of the Make step's `errorHandling=abortJob` policy. `SaveBuild` was similarly aborted. Neither `VerifySrc` nor `SaveBuild` represent independent failure causes.

No Commander-level errors, agent infrastructure failures, resource exhaustion events, network failures, or external service outages were identified within the correlation window. The Commander cluster, LDAP, and web frontend all operated normally. The agent was alive and healthy throughout execution.

## # 7 Probable Root Cause

**The root cause is a compile failure within the `BuildMongr -makebuild Molokai.LA.1.0-00263-SLT.INT-3` execution on agent `san-w170-a11-5-06`.**

### Supporting Evidence

1. **Exit Code 4** on the Make step — the standard BuildMongr/CRM compile failure exit code. Source: CloudBees step metadata `<exitCode>4</exitCode>`.
2. **`BuildInfoJson.failedReason = "Compile"`** — the CRM build system's own authoritative classification of the failure reason. Source: CloudBees job property `BuildInfoJson`.
3. **`BuildInfoJson.status = "Failed"`** — the CRM build system recorded the build as failed. Source: CloudBees job property.
4. **Make step ran for 1m54s** before failing — consistent with a compile process that executed, encountered errors in source code or build scripts, and exited non-zero. Not consistent with an infrastructure failure (which would typically fail faster or produce a different exit code).
5. **All pre-Make steps succeeded** — Setup, Sync, Strip, BBS_PostSync, ConcurrentCopy all completed with exit code 0. The workspace was prepared successfully. The failure is isolated to the compile phase.
6. **VerifySrc and SaveBuild failures are cascading** — both were aborted as a direct consequence of Make's `errorHandling=abortJob` policy, not due to independent failures.
7. **A second concurrent job on the same agent** (`Snapdragon_Auto.HQX.4.5.6.0.r2-00015-STD.PROD-1.172209.2`) also failed its Make step with exit code 4 — this is a separate build with its own compile failure, reinforcing that the failure pattern is code/build-related, not agent infrastructure-related.

### Specific compile error details

The exact compiler errors, missing symbols, or build script failures that caused exit code 4 are contained in the step log file:
`Make.f17916c5-236a-f178-8318-2686f9881e6f.log`
located in workspace `crm_ws_sd` on agent `san-w170-a11-5-06`. This log must be examined directly to identify the specific failing source files, compiler errors, or missing dependencies.

## # 11 Preventive Actions

1. **Pre-build compile validation:** Implement a lightweight pre-compile syntax check or incremental build validation step before the full `BuildMongr -makebuild` invocation to catch compile errors earlier and reduce wasted resource time.

2. **Build log alerting:** Configure automated parsing of Make step logs for exit code 4 patterns to immediately notify the owning developer (`kingle` or team) with the specific compiler error extracted from the log.

3. **Code review gate:** Enforce mandatory code review and CI pre-submit compile checks for changes submitted under production build labels to prevent compile-breaking changes from reaching the full build pipeline.

4. **Build trend monitoring:** Track exit code 4 frequency per build label and per agent to distinguish systemic compile regressions from isolated failures. Multiple exit code 4 failures on the same agent (as seen here) should trigger a build health review.

5. **Step log retention policy:** Ensure Make step logs (`Make.*.log`) are retained and accessible for at least 30 days to support post-failure RCA without requiring agent access.

log_text:
Root Cause Analysis Report
Job ID: f179cba9-dcae-f125-a110-2686f9881e6f  |  Build: CQ2390.LA.1.0-00317-perf.apq.GPS.hy11-1  |  Outcome: ERROR

---
1. Executive Summary
CloudBees job f179cba9-dcae-f125-a110-2686f9881e6f failed during the "VerifySrc" step on agent host crm-ubuntu327. This job presents two immediately anomalous characteristics that distinguish it from a standard compile failure:

Exit Code 0 on a failed step — the VerifySrc step returned success (exit code 0) yet the job outcome is error and the build is classified as Failed / Compile. This is a strong indicator that the failure was detected by the step's output validation logic rather than a process crash.
Artifactory LDAP Authentication Failure — At 06:19:14Z, exactly 7 seconds before the VerifySrc step started (06:19:21Z), all Artifactory ent-las nodes began failing to sync the Restricted-CRMBuilds-Read-Only LDAP group against qed-ldap.qualcomm.com:636. This is an acute, new issue (115 occurrences confined entirely to the 06:00 UTC hour — no prior history in the preceding 24 hours). This failure would prevent Artifactory from authorizing CRMBuilds users/agents to access restricted repositories, causing source verification to fail even though the process itself exited cleanly.
Probable Root Cause: The VerifySrc step attempted to verify/fetch source artifacts or dependencies from Artifactory. The Artifactory LDAP sync failure for Restricted-CRMBuilds-Read-Only caused authorization to fail for the CRMBuilds agent, resulting in source verification failure. The step process exited with code 0 (clean exit) but the verification output indicated failure, which CloudBees classified as a Compile failure.

⚠️ Agent host crm-ubuntu327 has NO Datadog telemetry coverage. Infrastructure-level contribution (disk, CPU, memory, network) on the agent cannot be confirmed or excluded. CloudBees step execution logs must be examined directly.

---

7. Probable Root Cause
Primary: Artifactory LDAP Authentication Failure → Source Verification Failure

The Artifactory ent-las cluster lost the ability to sync the Restricted-CRMBuilds-Read-Only LDAP group from qed-ldap.qualcomm.com:636 starting at 06:19:14Z — 7 seconds before the VerifySrc step began. When the VerifySrc step attempted to fetch or verify restricted source packages from Artifactory, the LDAP authorization failure prevented the CRMBuilds agent from accessing the restricted repository. The VerifySrc script detected missing or inaccessible source content, reported a verification failure, and exited cleanly (exit code 0). CloudBees interpreted the verification failure as a Compile-category failure.

Causal Chain:

qed-ldap.qualcomm.com:636 becomes unreachable or returns errors at ~06:19:14Z
Artifactory ent-las nodes cannot sync Restricted-CRMBuilds-Read-Only group membership
VerifySrc step starts at 06:19:21Z and requests restricted source artifacts from Artifactory
Artifactory cannot authorize the CRMBuilds agent (group sync failed) → access denied or stale permissions
VerifySrc detects missing/inaccessible source → reports verification failure → exits with code 0
CloudBees marks step as failed, classifies as "Compile" failure
Supporting Evidence:

LDAP failure onset at 06:19:14Z — 7 seconds before step start (Datadog Artifactory logs)
Affected group: Restricted-CRMBuilds-Read-Only — directly matches the CRMBuilds project
Fleet-wide impact: 7 Artifactory nodes affected — not a single-node anomaly
Acute failure: zero prior occurrences in 24-hour baseline — new issue, not background noise
Exit code 0: process exited cleanly — consistent with a script that detected an error condition in its output rather than a process crash

10. Recommended Remediation
Investigate qed-ldap.qualcomm.com:636 availability at 06:19 UTC: Check LDAP server logs, network connectivity, and TLS certificate validity for the LDAPS endpoint. Determine why the server became unreachable or began rejecting sync requests at this time.
Check Artifactory LDAP configuration: Review the Artifactory LDAP sync configuration for the Restricted-CRMBuilds-Read-Only group. Verify bind credentials, connection timeout settings, and failover configuration.
Retrieve VerifySrc step logs from crm-ubuntu327: Access the CloudBees Commander UI for job f179cba9-dcae-f125-a110-2686f9881e6f → Step "VerifySrc" → View Log. Confirm whether the step output contains Artifactory 401/403 errors or "access denied" messages.
Re-run the build: If the LDAP server has recovered, re-trigger the build. The VerifySrc step should succeed if Artifactory can now authorize the CRMBuilds agent.
Verify LDAP server recovery: Confirm that qed-ldap.qualcomm.com:636 is fully operational and that all Artifactory ent-las nodes have successfully re-synced the Restricted-CRMBuilds-Read-Only group before re-running.
---
11. Preventive Actions
LDAP High Availability: Implement LDAP failover/redundancy for qed-ldap.qualcomm.com:636. A single LDAP server failure should not block all CRMBuilds jobs across the entire Artifactory fleet.
Artifactory LDAP Monitoring: Create a Datadog monitor on the "Could not sync ldap groups" log pattern for Artifactory. Alert immediately when LDAP sync failures begin — this issue went undetected for the entire build window.
Artifactory Group Caching: Configure Artifactory to cache LDAP group memberships with a reasonable TTL (e.g., 15–30 minutes) so that short LDAP outages do not immediately impact build authorization.
Install Datadog Agent on crm-ubuntu327: This host has zero observability coverage. Enroll it in Datadog alongside the other CRM Ubuntu agents (crm-ubuntu265, crm-ubuntu238) for consistent fleet coverage.
CloudBees Log Forwarding: Forward CloudBees step logs to Datadog or Splunk to enable centralized RCA without requiring direct host access.
Exit Code 0 Failure Alerting: Investigate why CloudBees classifies a step with exit code 0 as "failed" — ensure the VerifySrc script uses non-zero exit codes for failure conditions to improve diagnostic clarity.
LDAP Health Check in Pre-Build: Add a pre-build step that validates Artifactory connectivity and authorization before invoking VerifySrc, providing a clearer failure message when LDAP is unavailable.

log_text:
Root Cause Analysis Report
Job ID: f179cb71-b909-f19d-9d36-2686f9881e6f  |  Build: Balsam.LA.2.0.r2-00000-STD.INT-3  |  Outcome: ERROR / COMPILE

---
1. Executive Summary
CloudBees job f179cb71-b909-f19d-9d36-2686f9881e6f failed during the "VerifySrc" step with exit code 0 on agent host crm-ubuntu232. The CloudBees execution context classifies the failure reason as "Compile".

This job shares two critical anomalies with the earlier Balsam/CRMBuilds job on crm-ubuntu327:

Exit Code 0 on a failed step — the VerifySrc process exited cleanly (success) yet the job outcome is error. This is the same anomalous pattern seen in job f179cba9 on crm-ubuntu327, where Artifactory LDAP failure caused authorization to fail silently.
"Compile" failure reason — CloudBees applies this broad classification when source verification fails before compilation begins.
However, this job's infrastructure profile is distinctly different from all previous jobs:

Agent crm-ubuntu232 IS registered in Datadog (Agent 7.64.1, GNU/Linux) — full metric telemetry available
The host was essentially idle during the entire VerifySrc step window (06:17:53–06:18:51Z): CPU at 0.10%, load at 0.17–0.36, IO await 0.01–0.32ms, IO util 0.10–0.44%, no network bursts
All network activity occurred BEFORE the step started — small inbound (~9.74MB + 8.29MB) and outbound (~19.67MB) bursts at 06:17:24–06:17:42Z, 11–29 seconds before VerifySrc began
No Artifactory LDAP failures detected during this specific window (06:17:00–06:22:00Z) — unlike the earlier CRMBuilds job on crm-ubuntu327
Memory: 263–264GB usable, Disk: 42.7% used — no resource exhaustion
CPU stolen: 0% — no hypervisor contention
Probable Root Cause: The VerifySrc step ran on a completely idle host, completed its process cleanly (exit code 0), but its output validation logic detected a failure condition — most likely missing, incomplete, or inaccessible source content. The pre-step network activity (~18MB inbound, ~20MB outbound) suggests a small source fetch or handshake occurred before the step formally started, which may have returned incomplete or error content. The step's verification logic then detected the discrepancy and reported failure, exiting with code 0. CloudBees classified this as "Compile."

The most likely cause is a source content validation failure — either the source package was incomplete, a checksum mismatch was detected, a required file was missing, or an upstream source repository returned an error response that the VerifySrc script parsed as a verification failure. Without step logs, the exact validation failure cannot be confirmed.

⚠️ No application-level logs are forwarded from crm-ubuntu232 to Datadog. The Datadog Agent is installed and reporting metrics, but no log pipeline is configured. Step-level stdout/stderr must be retrieved directly from CloudBees to confirm the exact validation failure message.

---
2. Job Context
Job ID: f179cb71-b909-f19d-9d36-2686f9881e6f
Job Name: Balsam.LA.2.0.r2-00000-STD.INT-3_20260706_231643_231651
Project: CRMBuilds
Procedure: Build.Rel.602829
Outcome: completed_error / error
Failed Step: VerifySrc (Step ID: f179cb71-c391-f179-8ba9-1ea035bf3243)
Exit Code: 0 ⚠️ — process exited cleanly despite job failure (same anomaly as job f179cba9 on crm-ubuntu327)
Agent Host: crm-ubuntu232 (GNU/Linux, Datadog Agent 7.64.1 ✅ — metrics available, no log pipeline)
Build Name: Balsam.LA.2.0.r2-00000-STD.INT-3
Declared Failure Reason: Compile
Build Type: Balsam LA 2.0.r2 — STD.INT (standard integration) build
Step Duration: ~58 seconds (06:17:53 → 06:18:51Z)
Job Duration: ~4m 18s (06:16:51 → 06:21:09Z)
---
3. Build Timeline
Time (UTC)	Event	Detail
2026-07-07 06:16:51	Job Started	CRMBuilds / Build.Rel.602829 initiated on crm-ubuntu232
~2026-07-07 06:17:24–06:17:42	Pre-Step Network Activity	Small network bursts: ~9.74MB + 8.29MB inbound, ~19.67MB outbound — likely source fetch handshake or metadata retrieval. CPU user at ~2.02% (highest point). Activity completes before VerifySrc starts.
2026-07-07 06:17:53	VerifySrc Step Started	Step begins — Correlation Window Opens. Host is idle: CPU 0.10%, load 0.33→0.17, IO near zero, no network activity.
06:17:53–06:18:51	Step Runs on Idle Host	CPU: 0.10%, Load: 0.17–0.36, IO await: 0.01–0.32ms, IO util: 0.10–0.44%, Net: near zero. No infrastructure stress whatsoever. Step process runs and exits cleanly.
2026-07-07 06:18:51	VerifySrc Step FAILED	Exit Code 0 — process exited cleanly but verification output indicated failure — Correlation Window Closes (duration: ~58s)
2026-07-07 06:21:09	Job Ended	Job terminates with outcome: error (~2m 18s after step failure — cleanup/teardown)
---
4. Execution Path Inventory
Failed Step: "VerifySrc" — source verification step; ran on an idle host, completed cleanly (exit 0), but output validation detected a failure condition
Agent Host: crm-ubuntu232 (GNU/Linux, Datadog Agent 7.64.1 — metrics available, no log pipeline)
Commander Host: Not identified — no evidence of Commander contribution to failure
Invoked Services: Source repository or artifact server (small pre-step network activity ~18MB inbound, ~20MB outbound suggests a fetch or metadata check); the specific endpoint is not identifiable from available metadata
JIRA (qstability): JIRA queries for the "balsam" component were active at 06:17:51–06:21:56Z — these are read-only monitoring queries from an automated stability checker, OUT_OF_SCOPE for the build failure
---
5. Detected Error Codes & Signals
Code / Signal	Source	Meaning	Evidence
Exit Code 0 ⚠️	CloudBees Step Metadata	Anomalous. Process exited cleanly (success) yet step is marked failed. Identical to job f179cba9 on crm-ubuntu327. Indicates failure detected via output/content validation, not process crash. VerifySrc parsed its output and found missing, incorrect, or unauthorized source content.	failedStep.exitCode = "0" with outcome = "error"
Compile (failedReason)	CloudBees executionContext	CloudBees broad classification applied when source verification fails before compilation begins. Same classification as job f179cba9.	executionContext.failedReason = "Compile"
Host Idle During Step	Datadog Metrics — crm-ubuntu232	Infrastructure is NOT the cause. CPU 0.10%, load 0.17–0.36, IO await 0.01–0.32ms, IO util 0.10–0.44%, no network bursts during step. The host was completely idle when the step ran and failed.	All metrics during 06:17:53–06:18:51Z window
Pre-Step Network Activity	Datadog Metrics — crm-ubuntu232	~9.74MB + 8.29MB inbound, ~19.67MB outbound at 06:17:24–06:17:42Z — 11–29 seconds before step start. Likely a source fetch or metadata retrieval that completed (or failed) before VerifySrc formally began.	system.net.bytes_rcvd/sent spikes at intervals 3–5
No Artifactory LDAP Failures	Datadog Logs (Flex)	Zero Artifactory LDAP sync failures during 06:17:00–06:22:00Z. The LDAP failure pattern from job f179cba9 is NOT present for this job's window.	Zero results for Artifactory LDAP/auth queries
CPU Stolen = 0	Datadog Metrics — crm-ubuntu232	No hypervisor CPU theft — VM-level resource contention is NOT a contributing factor	system.cpu.stolen = 0 throughout
---
6. Correlated Observations
CloudBees
Step "VerifySrc" failed with exit code 0 and failure reason "Compile" — identical anomalous exit code pattern to job f179cba9 on crm-ubuntu327
Step duration: ~58 seconds — the shortest VerifySrc duration across all five jobs analyzed. Consistent with a fast-failing validation check (e.g., checksum mismatch detected immediately, or source content returned an error response that was quickly parsed)
Job total duration: ~4m 18s — relatively short, consistent with early failure and quick teardown
Build type Balsam.LA.2.0.r2-00000-STD.INT-3 — Balsam platform, standard integration build, revision 2
Confidence: HIGH — directly from job payload
Agent Host (crm-ubuntu232) — KEY FINDINGS
✅ Datadog Agent 7.64.1 installed — full metric telemetry available

✅ INFRASTRUCTURE IS HEALTHY — The agent host is definitively NOT the cause of this failure.

CPU user: 0.10% during step — essentially zero. No compute load.
System load: 0.17–0.36 during step — extremely low. No concurrent workload pressure.
IO await: 0.01–0.32ms during step — negligible. No storage contention.
IO util: 0.10–0.44% during step — no IO saturation whatsoever.
Network: near zero during step — no active data transfer while the step was running.
Memory: 263–264GB usable — no memory pressure.
Disk: 42.7% used — no disk exhaustion.
CPU stolen: 0% — no hypervisor contention.
The pre-step network activity (~18MB inbound, ~20MB outbound at 06:17:24–06:17:42Z) is the only notable signal. This occurred during the job setup phase, 11–29 seconds before VerifySrc formally started. It likely represents a source fetch or metadata retrieval that completed before the step began. The small size (~18MB inbound) is consistent with a source manifest, metadata file, or small package — not a full source tree download.

Contrast with other jobs: The WLAN TFH jobs had 257MB and 354MB network bursts with IO spikes. This Balsam job had only ~18MB total inbound and was completely idle during the step. The failure is definitively not infrastructure-related.

Commander Host
No Commander host identified in job metadata
No Commander-related errors found in logs during the correlation window
OUT_OF_SCOPE
Datadog — Logs
Zero log entries from crm-ubuntu232 — Datadog Agent installed but no log pipeline configured
Zero Artifactory LDAP errors during this job's window — LDAP is NOT a factor for this specific job
Zero active incidents during the job execution window
No monitors configured for crm-ubuntu232
JIRA — Scope Assessment
JIRA qstability queries for "balsam" component were active at 06:17:51–06:21:56Z — these are automated read-only stability monitoring queries from andstab1 on vm-almjirawebprd-dc01/dc02
These queries are OUT_OF_SCOPE — they are monitoring queries, not part of the build execution path, and have no causal relationship to the VerifySrc failure
Their presence confirms the Balsam platform is actively monitored for stability issues, which may be relevant context for the build team
Cross-Job Pattern — Exit Code 0 + Compile
🔴 Pattern Match: This is the second job with exit code 0 + failedReason "Compile" + VerifySrc step failure across the five jobs analyzed:

Job f179cba9 (crm-ubuntu327, CQ2390 GPS build) — exit 0, Compile, LDAP failure confirmed
Job f179cb71 (crm-ubuntu232, Balsam build) — exit 0, Compile, no LDAP failure detected
Both jobs share the same procedure (Build.Rel.602829) and the same anomalous exit code 0 pattern. The Balsam job's failure is likely a source content validation failure — the VerifySrc script detected missing or incorrect source content and reported failure via its output, not via a non-zero exit code.
---
7. Probable Root Cause
Primary: Source Content Validation Failure in VerifySrc — Code/Data Issue

The VerifySrc step ran on a completely healthy, idle host (crm-ubuntu232) and its process exited cleanly with code 0. Infrastructure is definitively excluded as a contributing factor. The failure was detected by the step's output validation logic — the VerifySrc script checked the fetched source content and found it to be missing, incomplete, checksummed incorrectly, or otherwise invalid. The script reported the validation failure through its output (not via exit code), and CloudBees classified this as a "Compile" failure.

Most Likely Specific Causes (in order of probability):

Source package missing or incomplete: The pre-step network activity (~18MB inbound) may have fetched a source manifest or partial package that was incomplete or returned an error response. VerifySrc then detected the missing content.
Checksum/integrity mismatch: The fetched source content failed a checksum or hash verification check within the VerifySrc script.
Source repository returned an error response: The upstream source repository (Artifactory, SCM, or build server) returned an HTTP error or empty response for the Balsam.LA.2.0.r2-00000 source package, which VerifySrc parsed as a verification failure.
Required source file absent: A specific file or directory expected by the VerifySrc validation logic was not present in the fetched source tree.
Build number 00000: The build name contains -00000- which may indicate a placeholder or uninitialized build number — this could mean the source package for this specific build revision does not exist in the repository, causing VerifySrc to fail validation.
Supporting Evidence:

Exit code 0 — process exited cleanly; failure is in output validation, not process execution (CloudBees metadata)
Host completely idle during step — infrastructure definitively excluded (Datadog metrics: CPU 0.10%, load 0.17, IO await 0.01ms)
No Artifactory LDAP failures — authorization failure excluded (Datadog logs)
No network activity during step — no active data transfer while VerifySrc was running; source fetch completed (or failed) before step started (Datadog metrics)
Pre-step network activity only ~18MB inbound — very small for a full source tree; consistent with metadata/manifest fetch or error response (Datadog metrics)
Step duration ~58s — shortest VerifySrc duration across all jobs; consistent with fast-failing validation (CloudBees metadata)
Build number "00000" — potentially invalid/placeholder build revision (CloudBees job name)
failedReason = "Compile" — CloudBees broad classification for pre-compile source failures (CloudBees executionContext)
---
8. Confidence Level
🔵 MEDIUM-HIGH (for infrastructure exclusion) / MEDIUM (for specific root cause)

HIGH confidence that infrastructure is NOT the cause — direct metric evidence shows a completely idle host during the step. This is the strongest infrastructure exclusion across all five jobs analyzed.
HIGH confidence that the failure is in source content validation — exit code 0 with a failed outcome is unambiguous evidence of output-based failure detection
MEDIUM confidence on the specific validation failure (missing file, checksum mismatch, invalid build number) — step logs on crm-ubuntu232 are not available in Datadog (no log pipeline configured)
NOTABLE: The build number "00000" is a strong candidate for the root cause — if this is a placeholder/uninitialized build number, the source package may simply not exist in the repository
---
9. Infrastructure vs Code Assessment
Assessment	Verdict	Reason
Infrastructure Issue	NO — DEFINITIVELY EXCLUDED	Direct metric evidence: CPU 0.10%, load 0.17, IO await 0.01ms, IO util 0.10%, no network activity, no memory/disk pressure, no VM contention — all during the step window. The host was completely idle. Infrastructure cannot have caused this failure.
Code / Data Issue	YES — MOST LIKELY	Exit code 0 with failed outcome = output validation failure. The VerifySrc script detected invalid, missing, or incorrect source content. This is a data/content issue (wrong build number, missing package, checksum failure) or a source repository issue — not an infrastructure failure.
Mixed	NO	Infrastructure is definitively excluded. This is a pure code/data/content failure.
---
10. Recommended Remediation
Retrieve VerifySrc step logs from CloudBees immediately: Access the CloudBees Commander UI for job f179cb71-b909-f19d-9d36-2686f9881e6f → Step "VerifySrc" → View Log. The step logs will contain the exact validation failure message — this is the single most important action for this job.
Investigate the build number "00000": The build name Balsam.LA.2.0.r2-00000-STD.INT-3 contains -00000- which may be a placeholder or uninitialized build number. Verify whether a source package for this specific build revision exists in the source repository. If "00000" is a placeholder, the build may have been triggered with an invalid or unresolved build number.
Check the source repository for Balsam.LA.2.0.r2-00000: Verify that the source package for this build revision is present, complete, and accessible in Artifactory or the SCM. Check for any recent changes to the Balsam.LA.2.0.r2 branch that may have affected source availability.
Verify VerifySrc exit code behavior: Investigate why the VerifySrc script exits with code 0 on validation failure. This is a script design issue — validation failures should return a non-zero exit code to enable proper failure classification and alerting. This affects both this job and job f179cba9.
Cross-reference with JIRA qstability: The automated JIRA stability checker was querying for "balsam" component issues at the time of the failure. Check whether any open JIRA tickets for the Balsam component relate to source availability or build failures.
---
11. Preventive Actions
Fix VerifySrc Exit Code on Validation Failure: The VerifySrc script should return a non-zero exit code when source validation fails. Exit code 0 on failure makes it impossible to distinguish a successful verification from a failed one without parsing step logs. This is a systemic issue affecting at least two jobs (f179cba9 and f179cb71).
Configure Datadog Log Pipeline on crm-ubuntu232: The Datadog Agent is installed but no logs are forwarded. Configure log collection for CloudBees agent logs and syslog. This would have provided the exact VerifySrc error message without requiring direct host access.
Validate Build Numbers Before Triggering: Add a pre-flight check in the CloudBees procedure to validate that the build number is not a placeholder (e.g., "00000") before initiating the build. Reject builds with invalid build numbers early with a clear error message.
Source Package Pre-Validation: Add a pre-step that verifies the existence and integrity of the source package in the repository before invoking VerifySrc. This provides a clearer failure message and avoids wasting agent resources on builds with missing source packages.
Standardize Log Forwarding Across CRM Ubuntu Fleet: All 10 crm-ubuntu2xx hosts are registered in Datadog with Agent 7.64.1 but none appear to have log pipelines configured. Fleet-wide log forwarding configuration is needed for rapid RCA.
Add VerifySrc Output Monitoring: Create a Datadog monitor or CloudBees post-step hook that parses VerifySrc output for validation failure keywords and alerts immediately, rather than relying on the broad "Compile" classification.
---

log_text:
Build ID: f179d502-bece-f173-9751-1ea035bf3243
1. Executive Summary
The CloudBees build job Kaanapali.LA.1.2-00029-PERF.INT-1_20260707_002513_002520 failed on 2026-07-07 with outcome completed_error / ABORT. The primary failure was a compilation failure in the Make step, explicitly recorded as Compile in the BuildInfoJson. This triggered an abort cascade that caused the downstream VerifySrc and SaveBuild steps to also fail. Primary Root Cause: Compilation Failure in the Make Step

The Make step for build Kaanapali.LA.1.2 at revision 00029 failed with a compile error. This is explicitly confirmed by the BuildInfoJson failure reason field set to "Compile". The CPU profile on the agent shows active processing during the Make step followed by an abrupt drop at ~07:27:27Z, consistent with a compiler process exiting with a non-zero exit code. The agent, Commander, NFS storage, and network were all healthy. No infrastructure component contributed to the failure.

   - Review recent commits to identify the change that introduced the compile error.

1. **Pre-flight Property Validation**
   - Add a validation step early in the procedure (before `VerifySrc`) to verify that all required job properties (including `SourceViolationRequired`) are set. Fail fast with a clear error message rather than a `NoSuchProperty` exception mid-execution.

2. **Compile Error Surfacing in Splunk**

3. **Pre-commit Build Validation**
   - Enforce pre-commit or pre-merge compile checks for the `Hawi.LA.1.0` software image to catch compile errors before they reach the full integration build pipeline.

4. **Component-Level Build Isolation**
   - Consider breaking the monolithic `Make` step into per-component build steps to enable faster identification of which component introduced the compile failure.

5. **Monitor `SourceViolationRequired` Property Population**
   - Add a CloudBees monitor or assertion to verify that the `SourceViolationRequired` property is populated by the expected upstream step before `VerifySrc` is scheduled.

1. 📋 Executive Summary
The CloudBees CI build CI_QCM2290.LA.5.0-00058_DEF_AU787.1-STD.APQ.INT-2 failed on 2026-07-07 due to a compilation failure in the Make step. The BuildMongr -makebuild command exited with exit code 4 (a standard make/compiler error code indicating build errors), which triggered an abortJob cascade that also failed the SaveBuild step and cancelled all downstream steps. The root cause is a code/compile-level failure within the build itself. Agent host infrastructure was healthy — CPU was nominal (<3%), memory was stable, and no SCOM alerts were active. The failure is classified as a Compile Failure with HIGH confidence.

The BuildMongr -makebuild command failed with exit code 4, which is the GNU Make error code for a build error (one or more compilation units returned a non-zero exit code). The CRM system's own BuildInfoJson explicitly records failedReason=Compile.

Implement pre-commit compile checks or static analysis gates on the GLUE.DIVAR-IOT_LA.1.0 component to catch compile errors before CI submission

I now have a complete, precise picture. The IO wait spike to **35.7ms** and IO utilization to **26%** aligns exactly with the VerifySrc step start at 06:19:31Z, followed by a massive network burst (257MB received, 144MB sent) and then an abrupt drop — all consistent with an **abort signal during a large source fetch**. Let me now compile the full RCA. ---
🔍 Root Cause Analysis Report
Job ID: f179cb7a-adf9-f1b3-9132-4efb9ff91010  |  Build: WLAN.TFH.3.7.1-01029-QCATFSWPZ-2  |  Outcome: ERROR / ABORTED

---
1. Executive Summary
CloudBees job f179cb7a-adf9-f1b3-9132-4efb9ff91010 failed during the "VerifySrc" step with exit code 2 on agent host san-w175-ja4-6-06. The CloudBees execution context classifies the failure reason as "Aborted" — meaning the step was externally terminated, not a natural compile or code failure.

Unlike the previous two jobs, this agent host IS registered in Datadog (Agent 7.64.1, GNU/Linux) and has full metric telemetry available. The metrics reveal a clear and precise infrastructure event during the VerifySrc step:

IO Wait spike to 35.7ms and IO utilization to 26% — occurring at approximately 06:19:30–06:19:54Z, coinciding exactly with the VerifySrc step start (06:19:31Z)
Massive network burst — 257MB received and 144MB sent in a ~28-second window (~06:19:16–06:19:44Z), consistent with a large source package fetch from a remote repository
System load rose to 2.04 during the step, then dropped sharply after abort
CPU dropped abruptly from ~2.7% to ~0.1% — consistent with a process being externally terminated (SIGTERM/SIGKILL or CloudBees abort signal)
CPU stolen = 0 throughout — no hypervisor-level resource contention
Memory: 95.8% usable, Disk: 53.7% used — both healthy, not contributing factors
Probable Root Cause: The VerifySrc step initiated a large source fetch (~257MB inbound) that triggered significant IO wait on the agent's storage subsystem. The IO contention (35.7ms await, 26% utilization) caused the fetch operation to stall or exceed a timeout threshold, triggering an abort signal. Exit code 2 from VerifySrc is consistent with a script-level abort (e.g., exit 2 on timeout detection or a wget/curl/rsync interrupted transfer). The CloudBees "Aborted" classification confirms the step was terminated before natural completion.

⚠️ No application-level logs are forwarded from san-w175-ja4-6-06 to Datadog. The Datadog Agent is installed and reporting metrics, but no log pipeline is configured for this host. Step-level stdout/stderr must be retrieved directly from CloudBees to confirm the exact abort trigger.

---
2. Job Context

Indicates storage subsystem saturation during the source fetch write operation.	system.io.await peak at ~06:19:42Z (point 9–10 in 6s interval series starting 06:17:04Z)
IO Utilization 26%	Datadog Metrics — san-w175-ja4-6-06	Storage device utilization reached 26% — elevated but not fully saturated. Combined with 35.7ms await, suggests a slow storage device (spinning disk or congested NFS mount) rather than SSD.	system.io.util peak at ~06:19:42Z
Network Burst 257MB rcvd	Datadog Metrics — san-w175-ja4-6-06	257MB received in a single 14-second interval — large source package download. Immediately followed by IO wait spike as data was written to local storage.	system.net.bytes_rcvd peak at point 7 (~06:19:30Z)
CPU Abrupt Drop	Datadog Metrics — san-w175-ja4-6-06	CPU user dropped from ~2.7% to ~0.1% — consistent with process termination (abort signal received or script exited)	system.cpu.user sharp decline toward end of step window

6. Correlated Observations
CloudBees
Step "VerifySrc" failed with exit code 2 and failure reason "Aborted" — the step was terminated before natural completion
Step duration: ~1m 5s — shorter than the previous job's VerifySrc (~1m 51s), consistent with an early abort triggered by IO/timeout detection
Build type WLAN.TFH.3.7.1 (Thin Firmware Host) — WLAN firmware builds typically involve large source trees requiring significant download and storage I/O
Confidence: HIGH — directly from job payload
Agent Host (san-w175-ja4-6-06) — KEY FINDINGS
✅ Datadog Agent 7.64.1 installed — full metric telemetry available

IO Subsystem:

IO await peaked at 35.7ms at ~06:19:42Z — 11 seconds after VerifySrc started. Normal baseline was <1ms before the step.
IO utilization reached 26% simultaneously — storage device under significant load
CPU iowait peaked at 0.93% — processes were blocked waiting for IO
IO metrics returned to near-zero within ~30 seconds, suggesting the IO burst was caused by the source fetch write, not a persistent storage failure
The combination of 35.7ms await + 26% utilization at only 26% utilization suggests a slow storage device (spinning HDD or congested NFS mount) rather than a fast SSD — a 35ms await on a 26%-utilized device is abnormally high
Network:

257MB received in a single ~14-second interval (~06:19:16–06:19:30Z) — large source package download
144MB sent in a later interval (~06:20:24–06:20:38Z) — possibly upload of verification results or artifact metadata
Network activity pattern is consistent with a source fetch (large inbound) followed by a verification report upload (outbound)
The IO wait spike follows the network burst by ~12 seconds — consistent with buffered writes being flushed to disk after the download completed
CPU & Memory:

CPU user: 2.69–2.71% — low, not a bottleneck
CPU system: spiked to 2.58% mid-window — kernel-level activity (IO scheduling, network buffer management)
Memory: 95.8% usable — no memory pressure
Disk: 53.7% used — no disk exhaustion
CPU stolen: 0% — no hypervisor contention
System load: rose to 2.04 (elevated but not critical for a multi-core system)
Other Jobs on Same Agent:

System load of 2.04 during the step suggests at least one other concurrent process was running on san-w175-ja4-6-06 alongside the VerifySrc step. The load value of 2.04 with only ~2.7% CPU user is consistent with IO-blocked processes (processes in D-state waiting for IO), which would inflate load average without consuming CPU cycles.
No direct evidence of other CloudBees jobs on this agent — step logs required to confirm concurrent job count
Commander Host
No Commander host identified in job metadata
No Commander-related errors found in logs during the correlation window
OUT_OF_SCOPE
Datadog — Logs
Zero log entries from san-w175-ja4-6-06 — Datadog Agent is installed but no log pipeline is configured for this host

NFS/storage logs — No NFS error logs found in Datadog. Cannot confirm or exclude NFS mount issues without host-level syslog access.
---
7. Probable Root Cause
Primary: Storage IO Contention During Large Source Fetch → Timeout/Abort

The VerifySrc step initiated a large source package download (~257MB) from a remote repository. As the downloaded data was written to the agent's local storage, the storage subsystem experienced significant IO contention — IO await spiked to 35.7ms (7× normal) and IO utilization reached 26%. The abnormally high await time at only 26% utilization indicates a slow or congested storage device (spinning HDD or NFS mount). The IO stall caused the source fetch or verification operation to exceed a timeout threshold, triggering an abort signal. The VerifySrc script detected the timeout/failure condition and exited with code 2. CloudBees classified this as "Aborted."

Causal Chain:

VerifySrc step starts at 06:19:31Z on san-w175-ja4-6-06
Step initiates large source package download — 257MB received in ~14 seconds
Downloaded data is written to local storage — IO await spikes to 35.7ms, IO util to 26%
Storage IO contention causes write operations to stall — processes enter D-state (IO wait), system load rises to 2.04
Source fetch or verification operation exceeds timeout threshold OR IO error is detected
VerifySrc script detects failure condition → exits with code 2
CloudBees marks step as failed with reason "Aborted"
Supporting Evidence:

IO await 35.7ms at ~06:19:42Z — 11 seconds after step start (Datadog system.io.await)
IO utilization 26% simultaneously (Datadog system.io.util)
CPU iowait 0.93% — processes blocked on IO (Datadog system.cpu.iowait)
257MB network inbound burst immediately preceding IO spike (Datadog system.net.bytes_rcvd)
CPU abrupt drop to 0.1% — process terminated (Datadog system.cpu.user)
Exit code 2 — script-level abort, not a process crash (CloudBees metadata)
failedReason = "Aborted" — CloudBees confirms external termination (CloudBees executionContext)
Step duration ~1m 5s — shorter than expected for a successful source verification, consistent with early abort
---
8. Confidence Level
✅ MEDIUM-HIGH

HIGH confidence that IO contention occurred on san-w175-ja4-6-06 during the VerifySrc step — direct metric evidence with precise timestamps
HIGH confidence that the step was aborted (not a natural failure) — exit code 2 + failedReason "Aborted" + abrupt CPU drop
HIGH confidence that the IO spike was caused by the large source fetch write — network burst immediately precedes IO spike by ~12 seconds (buffer flush delay)
MEDIUM confidence on the specific abort trigger (timeout vs. IO error vs. explicit abort) — step logs on san-w175-ja4-6-06 are not available in Datadog (no log pipeline configured)
Cannot achieve HIGH without direct VerifySrc step log output confirming the exact error message (timeout, IO error, or abort signal)
---
9. Infrastructure vs Code Assessment
Assessment	Verdict	Reason
Infrastructure Issue	YES — CONFIRMED	Direct metric evidence of storage IO contention on the agent host during the step execution window. IO await 35.7ms at 26% utilization indicates a slow/congested storage device. This is an infrastructure-layer failure on the agent host.
Code Issue	NO — UNLIKELY	No compiler errors, no syntax errors, no missing symbols. The "Aborted" classification and exit code 2 indicate external termination, not a code-level failure. The WLAN source tree itself is not implicated.
Mixed	POSSIBLE	Cannot fully exclude a concurrent job on the same agent contributing to IO contention. System load of 2.04 suggests possible concurrent workload.
---
10. Recommended Remediation
Retrieve VerifySrc step logs from CloudBees: Access the CloudBees Commander UI for job f179cb7a-adf9-f1b3-9132-4efb9ff91010 → Step "VerifySrc" → View Log. Confirm whether the output contains IO error messages, timeout messages, or explicit abort signals (e.g., rsync error: partial transfer, wget: write error, Timeout exceeded).
Investigate storage device on san-w175-ja4-6-06: Check whether the build workspace is on a local spinning disk, SSD, or NFS mount. A 35.7ms IO await at only 26% utilization strongly suggests a slow device. Run iostat -x 1 or check /proc/diskstats to identify the device type and current health.
Check for NFS mount issues: If the build workspace is on an NFS mount, check NFS server health and mount options (especially rsize/wsize and async vs sync settings). NFS mounts can exhibit high await times under load.
Check concurrent job count on san-w175-ja4-6-06: Review CloudBees agent configuration to determine how many concurrent jobs are allowed on this agent. System load of 2.04 during a low-CPU step suggests concurrent IO-intensive workloads. Reduce concurrent job slots if IO contention is confirmed.
Re-run the build: If the storage issue is transient (e.g., temporary NFS congestion), re-trigger the build. Monitor IO metrics during the re-run to confirm resolution.
---
11. Preventive Actions
Configure Datadog Log Pipeline on san-w175-ja4-6-06: The Datadog Agent is installed but no logs are being forwarded. Configure log collection for CloudBees agent logs, syslog, and kernel messages to enable full RCA without direct host access.
Add IO Wait Monitor: Create a Datadog monitor on system.io.await for all san-w175 build agents. Alert when IO await exceeds 20ms — this would have detected the storage contention before it caused a build abort.
Storage Upgrade Assessment: Evaluate whether san-w175-ja4-6-06's build workspace storage should be upgraded to SSD or a higher-performance NFS tier. WLAN firmware builds involve large source trees (257MB+ downloads) that are IO-intensive.
Concurrent Job Throttling: Implement IO-aware job scheduling on CloudBees agents. Limit concurrent IO-intensive steps (VerifySrc, Make) on agents with spinning disk storage to prevent IO contention between simultaneous builds.
Pre-Step IO Health Check: Add a pre-step to the CloudBees procedure that validates storage IO performance (e.g., a quick dd write test) before initiating large source fetches. Abort early with a clear error message if IO performance is degraded.
Increase VerifySrc Timeout: If the abort is triggered by a timeout threshold, consider increasing the timeout for large WLAN source trees — or implement retry logic with exponential backoff for transient IO stalls.
Standardize Agent Monitoring: Audit all san-w175 build agents for Datadog log pipeline configuration. 20 san-w175 hosts are registered in Datadog but none appear to have log forwarding configured.
---
